def: The answer of a defendant in matter of fact to a plaintiff'ssurrejoinder.
To assert again or anew; to maintain after an omission to doso.Let us hope . . . we may have a body of authors who will reassert ourclaim to respectability in literature. Walsh.
A five-pointed star, resembling five alphas joined at theirbases; -- used as a symbol.
Coming, or made, or done, once every half week; as, asemiweekly newspaper; a semiweekly trip.-- n.
A woven band of cotton or flax, used for reins, girths, bedbottoms, etc.

A telephonic instrument for office or other similar use, havinga sound-magnifying device enabling the ordinary mouthpiece to bedispensed with. Much use has been made of it for overhearing, or forrecording, conversations for the purpose of obtaining evidence foruse in litigation.
To wrap up; to cover. Fairfax.
Moving in a circle, or spirally; revolving; whirling around.
